positives: Incredible noise isolation across broad frequency range
8+ hours battery life with ANC enabled
High-res codec support: LDAC and LC3
positives_detail: They attenuate a wide range of frequencies including plane engine rumble and electrical noise, resulting in market-leading noise isolation.
They can last upwards of eight hours on a single charge with the ANC enabled, enabling all-day use.
They support high-res streaming codecs like LDAC and LC3, enabling high-quality wireless audio.

positives: Excellent ANC attenuates airplane cabin and car engine rumble
12+ hours battery life with ANC enabled
positives_detail: Their ANC effectively reduces noise from airplane cabins and car engine rumble for a quiet listening environment.
They deliver over 12 hours of playback per charge with ANC on, making them ideal for long trips.
They support Bluetooth multipoint pairing with up to three devices simultaneously for seamless switching.
negatives: Passive isolation weaker than Sony WF-1000XM6
negatives_detail: Their passive isolation with silicone tips is less effective at blocking ambient noise compared to the Sony WF-1000XM6.

positives: Warm sound profile out of the box
7+ hours battery life with ANC enabled
positives_detail: They carry an IPX4 rating, protecting them against splashes and light rain during workouts.
Their default sound profile is warm and well-balanced, suitable for a variety of audio content.
They deliver just over seven hours of playback per charge with ANC on for solid daily use.
They support three-way Bluetooth multipoint, allowing connection to multiple devices at once.
negatives: Battery life shorter than upper mid-range peers
negatives_detail: Their ANC performance is a step down compared to the Technics EAH-AZ100 in high-noise environments.
At just over seven hours per charge with ANC on, they offer less runtime than higher-end models.

positives: 7.5-hour battery life plus 3.7 extra charges in case
Balanced sound profile out of the box
Superior mic performance isolating voice from background noise
positives_detail: They achieve 7.5 hours of continuous playback with ANC on and provide an additional 3.7 charges in their case.
Their default tuning offers a well-balanced sound that suits a wide range of audio without EQ adjustments.
Their microphone effectively separates voice from ambient noise, making calls clearer in busy settings.
negatives: ANC less effective than Technics EAH-AZ80
negatives_detail: Their ANC performance does not match the Technics EAH-AZ80, offering a lower level of noise reduction.

records.raw_product_name: Anker Soundcore Space A40 Truly Wireless
positives: ANC performance punching above price category
8-hour battery life with ANC on and 4 extra case charges
positives_detail: Their noise-cancelling performance exceeds expectations for the budget category, reducing ambient noise effectively.
They last around eight hours per charge with ANC on and offer four additional charges in the case.
They support pairing with multiple devices simultaneously for quick switching between phone and laptop.
negatives: No stability fins, may slip out during workouts
negatives_detail: They lack stability fins, making them less secure and prone to slipping out during vigorous movement.

positives: Lightweight, comfortable fit for day-to-day use
7+ hours battery life plus 4 extra charges in case
positives_detail: They offer adaptive ANC alongside LDAC support, uncommon features at this price point.
They are very lightweight and comfortable, suitable for prolonged daily wear.
They provide just over seven hours of playback per charge and four additional charges in the case.
negatives_detail: Their warm, bass-heavy default tuning includes a treble roll-off that can make voices lack sparkle.

positives: Noise isolation performance on par with top-tier earbuds
H2 chip enables seamless Apple integration and Spatial Audio
positives_detail: Their noise isolation matches that of the Sony WF-1000XM6, delivering excellent ambient blocking.
They achieve up to nine hours of continuous playback per charge with ANC enabled.
Their H2 chip provides easy pairing and switching between Apple devices along with Spatial Audio support.
negatives: No manual equalizer for sound customization
negatives_detail: They lack a graphic EQ for manual tuning, restricting adjustments of bass and treble.
Many features, such as advanced pairing and Live Translation, are only available on Apple devices.

positives_detail: They deliver strong active noise cancellation comparable to higher-end models.
They feature robust materials and construction for durable use.
Their microphone captures clear voice audio with decent noise rejection.
They support a range of aptX codecs for high-quality wireless audio.
negatives: Battery life shorter than Sony WF-1000XM6
ANC performance lower than Sony WF-1000XM6
negatives_detail: Their battery life and ANC runtime fall short of the Sony WF-1000XM6 in head-to-head tests.
Their noise cancellation is effective but does not match the Sony WF-1000XM6’s top-tier performance.

positives_detail: They offer a secure and stable fit, reducing the chance of slipping during use.
Their microphone provides clear voice capture, outperforming many budget alternatives.
They include virtual surround sound modes to enhance immersion.
negatives: Less comfortable than Anker Space A40
negatives_detail: They are reported to be less comfortable and more rigid compared to the Anker Soundcore Space A40.

records.raw_product_name: JLab Audio GO Air POP True Wireless
positives_detail: They come at a lower price point than many competing true wireless models, offering good value.
negatives: No ANC or companion app support
negatives_detail: They lack active noise cancellation and do not include a companion app for EQ or features.

positives: Balanced sound profile out of the box
positives_detail: They achieve longer continuous battery life per charge compared to the Anker Soundcore P31i.
Their default tuning offers a balanced sound profile without additional EQ adjustments.
negatives_detail: They do not include active noise cancellation.
Their microphone does not isolate voice from background noise as effectively as peers.

positives: 45 minutes playback from 3 minutes fast charge
positives_detail: Delivers 88% average noise reduction across all frequencies
Achieved a 4.8 out of 5 MDAQS sound quality score
Battery life hit 9 hours and 41 minutes in our testing
Fast charging delivers 45 minutes of playback from just 3 minutes of charging
Measured 90% average noise reduction, the best ANC recorded
Battery life jumped to 8 hours and 42 minutes in our testing
negatives: No EQ; sound varies with volume due to LDEQ tuning
negatives_detail: Most features require Apple devices; Android users get only basic Bluetooth support
Lacks EQ and sound signature changes noticeably with listening volume due to Apple’s LDEQ tuning

positives_detail: Supports LDAC and LC3 codecs for high-resolution and efficient audio
Battery life hit 10 hours and 40 minutes in our testing, one of the longest at this price
negatives: Stock tuning is dark and bass-heavy
negatives_detail: The default sound tuning leans dark and bass-heavy without EQ adjustments

positives_detail: Achieved 84% average noise reduction in ANC testing
Features IP57 water resistance
Scored 4.8 out of 5 in MDAQS Overall sound quality tests
negatives_detail: Battery life measured at 6 hours, which is below average for this price
Lacks true multipoint functionality, limiting seamless multi-device use

Battery life stretched to 10 hours on a single charge and 45 hours total with case
Includes a built-in heart rate monitor using optical sensor technology
negatives_detail: Does not support Bluetooth multipoint
Audio quality noticeably degrades when ANC is turned off

positives_detail: Achieved a 4.8 out of 5 MDAQS Overall score

Battery life measured at 6 hours and 17 minutes with ANC on

negatives: Battery life fell short of 12-hour rating
negatives_detail: Battery life was 6 hours 17 minutes in testing, below the rated 12 hours
ANC at 80% is solid but not best in class

Includes a transmitter case for audio over aptX Adaptive from 3.5mm or USB-C sources
Features Carbon Cone drivers and 32-bit DSP

The charging case scratches easily
EQ interface uses unlabeled frequency bands

positives_detail: Scored 4.8 out of 5 in MDAQS Overall testing
Offers LDAC and Bluetooth multipoint support
Battery life hit 5 hours and 42 minutes with ANC on
negatives: Case display more novelty than essential
negatives_detail: Spatial audio feature was underwhelming in practice
The built-in case display serves more as a novelty than a daily utility

positives_detail: Features IPX4 water resistance
Offers long battery life
Provides good active noise cancellation
negatives_detail: Not recommended for running due to lack of secure fit

Offers active noise cancellation under $30

positives: Comparable ANC and sound quality at mid-range price
positives_detail: Offers ANC and sound quality comparable to premium models at mid-range cost
Provides outstanding active noise cancellation
Delivers fantastic battery life
Delivers exceptional sound quality using xMEMS drivers
Includes a robust array of premium features
Delivers very solid performance across tests
negatives_detail: Earbuds are quite large, which may impact fit for some users

positives: Upgrades to ANC and sound quality
positives_detail: Sony shrank the WF-1000XM5 by 25 percent compared to the WF-1000XM4, resulting in a smaller, more comfortable fit.
The WF-1000XM5 feature larger drivers that deliver more detailed and dynamic audio along with upgraded active noise cancellation performance.
They provide much clearer voice call performance compared to previous models.
negatives: Glossy texture can make them harder to remove from case
Sony’s bonus features aren’t as seamless as those of its competitors
negatives_detail: The WF-1000XM5’s glossy finish on the case can make the earbuds harder to grip and remove.
Users report that Sony’s extra software features and integrations aren’t as seamless or user-friendly as those offered by competing earbuds.

positives_detail: The earbuds feature a standout yellow design that immediately draws attention.
They include a fun, super compact carrying case that’s one of the smallest we’ve tested.
Their sound quality outperforms expectations for the price point, delivering expressive, enjoyable audio.
negatives_detail: These earbuds are not widely available in retail channels, making them harder to find.
The plastic carrying case can show scuff marks easily with regular use.

positives_detail: They offer the best noise-canceling performance available in a set of earbuds, delivering exceptional ANC throughout the day.
The earbuds come with three ear tips and three stabilizer sizes for a secure fit across different ear shapes.
Bose added a wireless charging feature to the case for convenient recharging.
negatives_detail: The second-gen earbuds maintain a bulky, chunky design nearly identical to the first-generation model.
With around six hours of battery life (ANC on), their runtime is merely average compared to competitors.

positives: Adds ANC, transparency modes, spatial audio
positives_detail: The Powerbeats Pro 2 charging case now supports wireless charging for convenient top-ups.
They introduce active noise cancellation, a natural-sounding transparency mode, and support spatial audio.
The earbuds are lighter and the charging case is noticeably smaller than the original Pro model.
Built-in sensors enable heart rate monitoring directly from each earbud during workouts.
The Powerbeats Pro 2 deliver great audio performance for music playback.
Despite the new features, Beats kept the price unchanged from the first-generation Pro model.
negatives: Heart rate is not that useful for iOS users
negatives_detail: The charging case remains bulky and large, making it less pocket-friendly.
On iOS devices, heart rate data cannot be broadcast to gym equipment, reducing the sensor’s utility.

positives_detail: The AirPods Pro 3 feature improved active noise cancellation and enhanced bass performance for better sound.
They offer longer battery life, delivering around eight hours of listening with ANC turned on.
A new heart sensor allows standalone workout tracking for 50 workout types in the iOS Fitness app.
Live Translation enables real-time language translation directly on the earbuds.
Despite the new upgrades, the AirPods Pro 3 maintain the same starting price as the previous model.
They carry an IP57 rating, offering protection against dust and water immersion.
New ear tips, including an extra XXS size, deliver a better fit and improved passive isolation.
The camera remote control feature provides a convenient hands-free way to capture photos.
negatives: If you’re hoping for traditional foam ear tips, this isn’t that
negatives_detail: The AirPods Pro 3 use silicone ear tips only and do not include traditional foam tips.

records.recommendation_type: Best earbuds for Samsung Galaxy phone owners
positives: Voice commands are simple and so useful
positives_detail: The Galaxy Buds 3 Pro deliver excellent audio fidelity, matching top-tier competitors.
Their transparency mode offers a crisp, natural passthrough of external sounds.
Hands-free voice commands allow simple, useful controls without a wake phrase.
They provide good voice call clarity even in noisy environments.
negatives_detail: The stemmed design is functional but lacks inspiration or unique styling.
Noise cancellation performance is midtier and behind leading ANC earbuds.
Without multipoint connectivity, they cannot connect to two devices simultaneously.

records.recommendation_type: Best earbuds for Google Pixel phone owners
positives: Smaller and lighter design is supremely comfortable
Very pleasant and detailed sound quality
positives_detail: Google downsized the Pixel Buds Pro 2 significantly, making them supremely comfortable in the ear.
They offer more powerful active noise cancellation than the original Pixel Buds Pro.
Their transparency mode provides crystal-clear audio passthrough.
The earbuds deliver very pleasant and detailed sound quality.
negatives: Google still refuses to add higher-quality Bluetooth codecs, limiting overall fidelity
Gemini Live feels more feeble than futuristic
negatives_detail: Their ANC performance still doesn’t match the level provided by Bose earbuds.
By limiting Bluetooth codecs to AAC and SBC, Google restricts maximum audio fidelity.
The Gemini Live feature feels underwhelming rather than groundbreaking.

records.recommendation_type: Best earbuds if you own both Apple and Android devices
positives: Better sound, ANC, and battery life than Studio Buds
positives_detail: Compared to the Studio Buds, the Studio Buds Plus deliver better sound, stronger ANC, and longer battery life.
The translucent case option stands out with a cool ‘90s vibe.
They offer improved voice call performance over the first-generation Studio Buds.
negatives: ANC and transparency fall short of the AirPods Pro 3
Doesn’t offer all Apple ecosystem tricks
No wireless charging or in-ear detection
negatives_detail: Their ANC and transparency modes are less effective than those on the AirPods Pro 3.
They lack several Apple-exclusive features, such as spatial audio and audio sharing.
They do not support wireless charging or in-ear detection auto-pause.

positives_detail: They deliver tremendous, detailed sound quality that stands above nearly all competitors.
The fourth-generation model addresses prior bugs and battery reliability issues for better day-to-day performance.
These earbuds support Auracast for next-generation LE Audio broadcast features.
negatives: Noise cancellation does the job, but isn’t anything special
Long-term durability remains to be seen
negatives_detail: With a price around $279, they may be too expensive for some buyers.
Their noise cancellation works adequately but doesn’t match top-tier ANC models.
Given past durability issues with this line, their long-term build quality remains uncertain.

records.recommendation_type: Best earbuds for voice calls on iPhone
positives: Voice isolation can noticeably improve call clarity
positives_detail: The AirPods 4 deliver upgraded audio quality with richer bass than the previous generation.
Voice isolation uses on-device machine learning to eliminate background noise and improve call clarity.
They integrate deeply with Apple’s ecosystem, offering features like new Siri head movements and Find My support.
negatives: No built-in speaker on the case
negatives_detail: These AirPods lack wireless charging functionality in the standard model.
The case does not include a built-in speaker for location tracking via Find My.
They do not support multipoint connectivity to pair with multiple devices at once.

records.recommendation_type: Best earbuds for voice calls on Android
positives: Voice commands are simple and so useful
positives_detail: They deliver terrific sound quality for both media and voice calls.
Their transparency mode provides a crisp, natural passthrough of ambient sounds.
Hands-free voice commands are simple and useful during calls.
The voice pickup technology ensures good call clarity even in noisy settings.
negatives_detail: The design is uninspired and resembles many other earbuds on the market.
Their noise cancellation performance is midtier and not as strong as top ANC earbuds.
They cannot maintain connections with multiple devices simultaneously due to lack of multipoint.

positives_detail: With one of the highest sound quality results in the group, the sound is somewhat bass-forward but still appeals to the masses with rich textures and a fun mix that never leans too hard in one direction.
It comes with active noise cancellation, which you can adjust by increasing or decreasing transparency.
It offers better-than-average phone calls for those who still use their phones for chatting.
negatives_detail: The Bose buds are bulky, protruding and extending longer than most earbuds.
The buds work better for larger ears, with some small-eared testers finding them hard to wear securely.
The Bose QuietComfort Ultra (Gen 2) Earbuds are expensive, costing $299, which may not fit tighter budgets.

positives_detail: Apple upped its AirPods Pro 2 game with richer, warmer, and more expansive sound, dialing up the noise cancellation to some of the best we've ever seen and improving on the previous generation.
We love everything the AirPods have to offer, including the ultra-impressive 12.8-hour battery life that keeps you engaged longer than most competitors.
negatives_detail: Android users don't get any of the standout features, and performance is slightly dulled, lacking the shine they experience when paired with iOS devices.
With a list price of $249, these earbuds may be considered costly for budget-conscious buyers.

positives_detail: They are among the lightest and most comfortable earbuds, making them great for extended listening and all-day errands.
“They create a really dynamic, fun sound, making the most of hip hop, R&B, and soul music,” says one tester.
With a battery life of 10 hours, the Pixel Buds Pro 2 offer long-lasting listening sessions.
negatives_detail: Call quality suffers in noisy environments, making phone calls less clear.
The app isn't available on iOS, so there is limited Apple functionality and switching between devices is a chore.

positives_detail: The Nothing Ear Wireless are super comfortable, sweat-resistant, and provide a stable fit.
They have a punchy and powerful sound when you turn up the volume without any of the frequencies becoming muddy.
Be it sound or call quality, the Nothing Ear are a delight to use.
negatives_detail: The Nothing Ear Wireless aren't the best at active noise cancellation, requiring a higher spend for better ANC.
They don't have true location tracking from the app, though the buds can emit a sound if dropped.

positives_detail: If affordability is top of mind but enjoying music is also important, the CMF Buds 2 Plus are ready to enter the chat.
They feature one of the longest battery lives we've seen, allowing for all-day listening.
They offer a comfortable stem shape that fits similarly to AirPods, making them wearable for hours.
They deliver an enjoyable soundstage with emphasized lows and mids, offering good sound for the price.
negatives_detail: The CMF isn't the best for making calls or joining meetings, especially in busy or loud backgrounds.
Although they offer better-than-average noise cancellation, their performance remains average compared to more expensive models.

positives_detail: Surprisingly capable at a delightfully cheap price, the JLab Go Pop+ earbuds are an excellent, low-commitment entry into the wireless world.
They offer a rich feature set, including touch controls, a transparency mode, an IPX4 water resistance rating, and a functional app.
A standout feature is the safe hearing mode that allows you to cap the volume, making them a compelling choice for teens.
negatives_detail: The fairly neutral mix works decently across genres, with sound that neither offends nor dazzles.
The Go Pop+ doesn't offer active noise cancellation, limiting noise-blocking capability.

positives_detail: The Sennheiser Momentum True Wireless 4 are sonic gold, with a rich, layered, and immersive sound across an expansive stage.
They offer better comfort than other versions.
The app is easy to navigate and is loaded with features.
negatives_detail: At a list price of $300, these earbuds carry a higher price tag than many competitors.
Users struggled with pairing and connectivity across multiple devices, making the setup needlessly complicated.

positives_detail: These buds feature a vibrant, bass- and treble-forward mix, making them an excellent choice for pop, hip-hop, and R&B.
The Samsungs are featherlight and super comfy for most, thanks to four ear tip sizes and a lightweight feel.
They offer above-average active noise cancellation, similar to the Google Pixel Buds Pro 2.
These ear-hugging earbuds are uber comfortable, with many testers forgetting they even have them on.
They remain stable during most activities, providing a secure open design.
They offer clear call quality, with voices remaining intelligible even in chaotic environments.
negatives_detail: They lack active noise cancellation, favoring transparency over noise isolation.
While the treble range is well-developed, the mids and bass are weaker than those of in-ear Bose options.
With a list price of $299, the Ultra Open are an expensive option compared to other open-design earbuds.
